topTargets.name: KC Concepcion
topTargets.reasoning: Best triple-board Tier 2 fit if he slides to 1.10: Consensus #8 Tier 2 (WR4, CLE landing spot), RSP DoT 86.3 (#5 in Waldman's class, 'first-round value'), and ADP 13.57 — all three boards align under the new 60/20/20 weighting. Owner directive says target a Consensus Tier 2 player at 1.10, and Concepcion is the highest-floor name realistically still on the board.

topTargets.name: Jonah Coleman
topTargets.reasoning: Cleanest Tier 2 RB likely to be there at 1.10: Consensus #10 Tier 2 (RB3, DEN), ADP 20.19. Pigskins have 7 RBs but 5 contracts expiring this year — RB room thins fast. Consensus-primary weighting (60%) makes a Tier 2 RB with a real NFL backfield more attractive than reaching for an RSP-only WR.

topTargets.name: Omar Cooper Jr.
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #12 Tier 2 (WR6, NYJ), RSP DoT 83.9 ('Deebo clone' upside, 'worth second-round consideration'), ADP 17.20. Tier 2 across all three boards under 60/20/20. Acceptable 1.10 fallback if Concepcion and Coleman are gone — still a Consensus Tier 2 selection per owner directive.

topTargets.name: Skyler Bell
topTargets.reasoning: Owner-directed 2.10 priority target. Consensus #33 (Free Agents tier, BUF) keeps the market price low, but RSP DoT 85.9 ('Ja'Marr Chase high-end comp') flags him as the elite arbitrage swing. With Bell well outside Consensus top 30 he should fall to 2.10 (pick 26 overall) without a trade-up — the new RSP zone for the Pigskins is Round 2.

topTargets.name: Emmett Johnson
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #13 Tier 2 (RB5, KC), RSP DoT 84.6 ('Harold Fannin trajectory, underrated talent'). Backup 1.10 option if the top-three Tier 2 fits all go 1.05-1.09; KC landing spot is a real fantasy environment. Also viable as a 2.10 pivot if Bell is gone.

capPosture: Cap-strapped at $42.7M used / $2.3M space — all three rookies effectively must go taxi (50% base hit) to avoid blowing the $45M ceiling, especially with a 28-man active roster already.

wildcard.name: Myles Montgomery
wildcard.reasoning: RSP #13 DoT 84.1, tagged 'MOST UNDERRATED skill talent in the 2026 class' and likely UDFA. Off Consensus top 50 entirely. Classic 3.10 RSP-zone swing for a high-RSP-affinity owner who has down-weighted RSP at 1.10 but kept it live for late-round lottery tickets — exactly the spot owner directive carves out.

summary: With the owner override to 60/20/20 (Consensus primary), the Pigskins use 1.10 on a Consensus Tier 2 player — Concepcion if he falls, otherwise Coleman or Cooper Jr. — and explicitly do NOT take Skyler Bell in Round 1. Round 2 becomes the RSP zone: Bell at 2.10 is the headline target given his Consensus #33 / RSP DoT 85.9 split, and because Bell sits outside Consensus top 30 he is highly likely to be there at 2.10 without a trade-up — recommend HOLDING 2.10 + future 3rd rather than packaging them. Round 3 (3.10) is reserved for a Waldman-flavored swing like Myles Montgomery, with all three picks ticketed for taxi to manage the $2.3M cap squeeze.
